name: Castronovo Chocolate (factory)
notes: Bean-to-bar maker. 555 Colorado Ave, Stuart, Florida. First US maker to import cacao from Colombia’s Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta.

name: BOHO Chocolate (maker)
notes: Organic bean-to-bar maker in Florence, Massachusetts. Sources via Uncommon/Meridian Cacao, Cacao Services, Invalsa.

name: SOMA Chocolatemaker
notes: Craft bean-to-bar maker, Distillery District, Toronto. Single-origin microbatches from ~20 countries.

name: Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta (Arhuaco)
notes: Aracataca, Magdalena, Colombia. Native cacao harvested by the Arhuaco on ancestral land. Sourced by Castronovo & BOHO.

name: Tumaco
notes: Nariño, Colombia. Pacific-coast cacao region sourced by Castronovo.

name: Kablon Farms
notes: Tupi, South Cotabato, Philippines. Single-estate cacao sourced by BOHO Chocolate.

name: Alto Beni (wild harvest)
notes: Bolivia. Wild-harvested cacao. Soma origin.

name: Fazenda Boa Vista
notes: Bahia, Brazil. Soma origin.

name: CONACADO Co-op
notes: Dominican Republic national co-op (San Francisco de Macorís area). Soma origin.

name: Hacienda Elvesia
notes: Dominican Republic. Soma origin.

name: La Red Guaconejo
notes: Nagua area, Dominican Republic. Soma origin.

name: Costa Esmeraldas
notes: Esmeraldas, Ecuador. Nacional cacao. Soma origin (also an early HCP-designated flavor).

name: Hacienda Victoria
notes: Guayas, Ecuador. Soma origin.

name: Camino Verde
notes: Balao, Guayas, Ecuador. Renowned fermentary. Soma origin.

name: PISA co-op ("El Negrito")
notes: Produits Des Iles SA, near Cap-Haïtien, Haiti. Soma origin.

name: El Negrito
notes: Yoro, Honduras. Soma origin.

name: Bachelors Hall Estate
notes: Portland Parish, Jamaica. Soma origin.

name: Akesson's Farm
notes: Sambirano Valley, Ambanja, Madagascar. Soma origin.

name: Somia / Ambolikapiky
notes: Sambirano Valley, Madagascar. Soma origins.

name: Lacandon
notes: Lacandon Jungle, Chiapas, Mexico. Soma origin.

name: Rancho La Joya
notes: Comalcalco, Tabasco, Mexico. Soma origin.

name: Cacao Bisiesto
notes: Nicaragua. Soma origin.

name: Ingemann Farms
notes: Nicaragua. Fine-flavor fermentary. Soma origin.

name: Marañón Valley (Fortunato No. 4)
notes: Jaén/Bagua, Peru. Rare white Nacional (Pure Nacional). Soma origin; also HCP-recognized.

name: Satipo
notes: Junín, Peru. Soma origin.

name: Ucayali River
notes: Pucallpa, Peru. Soma origin.

name: Apurímac
notes: VRAEM region, Peru. Soma origin.

name: Markham Farms
notes: Markham Valley, Papua New Guinea. Soma origin.

name: Kokoa Kamili
notes: Kilombero, Morogoro, Tanzania. Central fermentary. Soma origin.

name: Pinalum
notes: Malekula island, Vanuatu. Soma origin.

name: Chuao
notes: Aragua, Venezuela. Legendary appellation cacao. Soma origin.

name: Porcelana / Ocumare
notes: Ocumare de la Costa, Venezuela (Porcelana also from Sur del Lago, Zulia). Soma origins.

name: Patanemo
notes: Carabobo, Venezuela. Soma origin.

name: Alberto Adriani (El Vigía)
notes: Mérida, Venezuela. Soma origin.

name: Guasare
notes: Zulia, Venezuela. Soma origin.

name: Semuliki Forest
notes: Bundibugyo, Uganda. Soma origin; HCP-recognized flavor.

name: Ben Tre / Tien Giang
notes: Mekong Delta, Vietnam. Soma origins.

name: Malagos / Puentespina Farms
notes: Davao, Philippines. HCP #16 (2019) heirloom designee.

name: Kampura Farm
notes: Guatemala. HCP #17 (2023) designee — Rodrigo Sevilla.

name: Maya Mountain Cacao
notes: Toledo District, Belize. HCP-recognized heirloom cacao.

name: Zorzal Cacao
notes: Dominican Republic. Bird-reserve estate; HCP-recognized heirloom cacao.
